item.title: Research
item.description: Research Interests My research interests lie primarily at the intersection of physics and computer science. I’m interested in machine learning with a particular focus on its applications in physics. I’m also looking to contribute towards intersectional areas in quantum computing.

item.title: Neural Network Quantum States
item.description: In perhaps one of the most well-known treatises in physics — the Principia Mathematica, Isaac Newton tries to explain the motion of the Moon under the gravitational influence of both the Sun and the Earth. At the time, Newton argued that “it would exceed the force of human wit to consider so many causes of motion at the same time”. Physics has come a long way since the times of Newton, but this question, called the many-body problem, troubles us to this day.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Modelling Neutron Star Parameters using Artificial Neural Networks A Study Oriented Project under Dr Kinjal Banerjee, Department of Physics The Equation of State (EOS) of a neutron star enables us to deduce its structure and behaviour; the EOS is often parameterised by certain key Nuclear Matter Parameters (NMPs) which can be used to form the EOS and subsequently to deduce important properties about the star. In this project, we are attempting to develop a neural network-based model to predict neutron star properties such as its mass, radius and tidal deformability using a multivariate Gaussian distribution of the NMPs as the input.

item.title: About
item.description: Hi there! I’m Ameya. I’m a fourth-year undergraduate student at BITS, Pilani - K. K. Birla Goa Campus where I’m studying Physics and Computer Science. Currently, I am working with Irene Lopez-Gutierrez at TUM where I am looking at efficient optimization techniques for neural network quantum states and their generalizability to other common neural network architectures. At BITS, my research has been primarily supervised by Dr. Kinjal Banerjee in Nuclear Astrophysics where I am working on developing neural network-based approaches to predict neutron star properties and unveil correlations between nuclear matter parameters.
